summary: Linked several fighter translation units, primarily Kirby copy-special files and Purin SpecialN, by splitting Kirby's large SpecialNPk region into smaller source files, updating GALE01 splits/symbols, and switching configure.py entries from NonMatching to Matching. The PR also made supporting header/include fixes in fighter, item, and stage code. Review discussion recorded concern that some Kirby linkage relied on splitting and extern/data handling that may cause later maintenance issues, but another maintainer argued the linkage gain outweighed minor regressions.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so multiple Kirby copy-special TUs are built as Matching: ftkirbyspecialpikachu.c, ftKb_SpecialNKp.c, ftKb_SpecialNLk.c, ftKb_SpecialNSs.c, ftKb_SpecialNFx.c, and ftkirbyspecialfox.c.
Changed configure.py so src/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialN.c is Matching instead of NonMatching.

Reworked config/GALE01/splits.txt around Kirby: ftKb_Init and ftKb_SpecialN ranges were shortened, the old ftKb_SpecialNPk.c range was replaced by separate ftkirbyspecialpikachu, ftKb_SpecialNKp, ftKb_SpecialNLk, and ftKb_SpecialNSs ranges, and ftkirbyspecialfox gained .data/.sdata ranges.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to convert many formerly global ftKb_Init_* and ftPr_Init_* float/string constants into local @NNN symbols matching their new owning TUs.
Deleted the old 2212-line src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNPk.c and added smaller Kirby files for Pikachu/Pichu, Captain Falcon, Link/Young Link, and Samus copy-special logic.
Moved ftKb_SpecialNPk_800F9FD4 out of ftKb_SpecialN.c into the new ftkirbyspecialpikachu.c split, with a new ftkirbyspecialpikachu.h header.
Adjusted ftKb_Init.c and ftKb_Init.h includes/declarations to account for the new Kirby splits and reduced exported SpecialNPk declarations.

Modified ftkirbyspecialfox.c with include cleanup, added default switch cases, restored/added ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDC00 in the file, and changed some assert/report data references according to the review excerpt.
Added a static sdata2_ordering helper in ftPr_SpecialN.c containing ordered float/double constants to satisfy data ordering for matching.
Adjusted common declarations in src/melee/ft/forward.h and src/melee/ft/types.h, including adding forward declarations such as ArticleDynamicBones, ftDynamics, and KirbyHatStruct.
Made small include/signature support changes in Ness, item animation list, and several gr stage headers/sources.
Changed tools/functions.sh rename_tu so it searches src and docs but no longer searches asm.
smart_moves: The PR attacked linkage by cutting a large Kirby SpecialNPk compilation unit into copy-ability-sized TUs, making address ranges and matching status more granular.
The split was paired with explicit GALE01 split-range updates, avoiding a source-only change that would leave the build map inconsistent.
Converting constants from global ftKb_Init_* or ftPr_Init_* names to local @NNN symbols reflects the new TU ownership and avoids pretending local data is global after a split.
For Purin SpecialN, the sdata2_ordering helper is a targeted matching tactic: it forces the needed float/double literal ordering without implying those constants have meaningful global names.
Header changes mostly moved toward forward declarations and narrower includes, which is useful when newly linked TUs expose include-order or type-completeness problems.
The tooling tweak to rename_tu avoids renaming asm files, which is sensible once source files are being split/renamed for linked TUs.
decomp_lessons: When linking decompiled TUs, update all three layers together: configure.py Matching/NonMatching status, config/GALE01/splits.txt ranges, and config/GALE01/symbols.txt ownership/scope.
Splitting a large fighter file can unlock matching, but reviewers may reject or caution against splits that exist only to work around extern/local-data problems.
Do not leave local constants as globally named symbols after moving ranges between TUs; use local @NNN labels when the evidence indicates TU-local data.
Small data and small data 2 ordering can be as important as text matching; helper functions containing ordered literals may be acceptable when clearly used as a matching scaffold.
Newly linked TUs often require broad cleanup outside the target fighter, including forward declarations, item headers, stage headers, and tool scripts.
Kirby copy-ability code is especially sensitive to splitting strategy because many copied specials share state, motion IDs, constants, and item dependencies.
Reviewers may prefer more natural source organization over declaring a split file matched by importing externs for data that should belong to another TU.
naming_conventions: Kirby copy-special files use both historical lowercase names like ftkirbyspecialpikachu.c and ft-prefixed names like ftKb_SpecialNKp.c, ftKb_SpecialNLk.c, and ftKb_SpecialNSs.c.
Kirby function names in the changed region retain address-suffixed names such as ftKb_SpecialNKp_800FA588 and ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDC00 where semantic names are not yet known.
Local constants in symbols.txt use @NNN labels rather than fabricated global names when scoped to a TU.
Forward declarations are centralized in forward.h files for opaque struct use, while full type definitions remain in types.h or subsystem headers.
The PR preserves existing motion-state naming patterns such as ftKb_MS_PkSpecialN, ftKb_MS_PcSpecialN, ftKb_MS_LkSpecialNLoop, and ftKb_MS_ClSpecialNLoop.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Marked newly linked objects as Object(Matching, ...) in configure.py only after carving matching split ranges in config/GALE01/splits.txt.
Moved .text, .data, .sdata, and .sdata2 boundaries around 0x800F9FD4-0x800FEDD0 and 0x804D93E8-0x804D9474 to match newly separated Kirby TUs.
Used local @ symbols for constants at addresses such as 0x803CB6D8, 0x804D3DC8, 0x804D93E8, and the ftPr_Init small-data ranges.
Added explicit default cases in some ftkirbyspecialfox.c switch statements, likely to stabilize generated control flow for matching.
Used a static sdata2_ordering function in ftPr_SpecialN.c to emit constants in a required order, including M_PI_2, M_TAU, 4503601774854144.0, 0.2, 3 * M_PI_2, M_PI, and -0.05f.
Reduced include sets and added precise headers such as ftCommon/ftCo_AttackDash.h, trigf.h, lb/lbcommand.h, and mp/forward.h where needed to compile linked TUs.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Asked why string/assert data references were changed to externs, in a hunk where OSReport("ftToSpecialNFox::Caution!!!\n") and HSD_ASSERT(465, 0) were replaced by OSReport(ftKb_Init_803CB6D8).

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Said splitting this file and calling it matched with externs is not the way to go about this.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Said the data-only split file should not exist.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Commented that Kirby code needs more splitting in general and that linkage done this way could cause issues later.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Responded that linking at all outweighed the minor regressions and that the issues were not difficult to fix.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that revisit Kirby SpecialNPk or undo extern/data-only splits from PR 2592.
Search for ftKb_SpecialNFx.c and ftKb_SpecialNPk_Data.c to see whether they survived after review or were removed before merge.
Audit Kirby local @ symbols around 0x803CB6D8 and 0x804D93E8 for later semantic naming.
Search for other uses of sdata2_ordering helpers in fighter matching PRs.
Check whether ftPr_SpecialN.c remained Matching after subsequent symbol or data-ordering cleanup.
